readings.title: What Every Programmer Should Know About Memory
readings.description: The definitive reference on memory system performance (Ulrich Drepper, Red Hat, 2007). Covers DRAM internals, cache architecture, TLB behavior, NUMA effects, and practical optimization techniques. Section 3 on CPU caches and Section 6 on NUMA are directly relevant to this week's material on cache-line optimization and NUMA-aware allocation.

readings.title: Systems Performance: Enterprise and the Cloud (2nd Edition)
readings.description: Comprehensive systems performance methodology by Brendan Gregg (Addison-Wesley, 2020). Covers CPU scheduling, memory architecture, profiling tools (perf, flamegraphs), and the USE (Utilization, Saturation, Errors) methodology. The industry standard for performance engineering.

readings.title: Erik Rigtorp: Low Latency Guide
readings.description: Comprehensive guide to low-latency programming on Linux. Covers CPU isolation (isolcpus, nohz_full, rcu_nocbs), NUMA-aware allocation, huge pages, interrupt management, and kernel bypass. Essential for trading systems and real-time applications.

readings.title: Kernel Bypass in HFT
readings.description: Practical overview of kernel bypass technologies for low-latency trading: DPDK poll-mode drivers, Solarflare OpenOnload, and RDMA. Covers measured latency improvements from 10-50 microseconds (kernel stack) to 1-5 microseconds (kernel bypass).

readings.title: SUSE: CPU Isolation with nohz_full (Part 3)
readings.description: Deep dive into the nohz_full kernel parameter. Explains timer tick mechanics, RCU callback offloading, and measured latency improvements from tickless operation on isolated cores.

readings.title: Linux Kernel Parameters Documentation
readings.description: Official Linux kernel documentation on boot parameters including isolcpus, nohz_full, rcu_nocbs, processor.max_cstate, and other tuning options for CPU isolation and low-latency operation.

readings.title: Performance Analysis and Tuning on Modern CPUs
readings.description: Open-access book by Denis Bakhvalov (2020) covering CPU microarchitecture from a performance perspective: branch prediction, cache behavior, SIMD vectorization, and profiling with hardware performance counters. The chapters on branchless programming and voltage-frequency scaling are directly relevant to this week's exercises.

readings.title: Dennard Scaling (Wikipedia)
readings.description: History and physics of Dennard scaling (1974) and its breakdown around 2006. Explains why frequency scaling stopped at approximately 4-5 GHz and the industry shifted to multi-core architectures.

readings.title: Brendan Gregg: Linux perf Examples
readings.description: Authoritative guide to Linux perf tool usage. Covers perf stat (hardware counters), perf record/report (sampling profiler), and flamegraph generation from perf data.
